summary: R2 is accepted. The prior QA blocker is resolved: the checked-in default `pnpm --filter @monsoon/sim-core test` command now passes without reviewer-supplied `--testTimeout`, and focused default execution of `tests/sim-core-m2-economy-population.test.ts` also passes. Diff review confirms R2 only adds a narrow explicit 15000 ms timeout to the expensive property test and does not reduce `numRuns=24`, `seed=1531`, generated ranges, or invariant assertions. This acceptance is limited to M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-FIXTURE-001 fixture/content/compiler/runtime/sim-core validation; it does not approve M7 content lock and does not approve downstream client map rendering or hit testing.

tests_run.result: Branch was codex/m7-map-topology-fixture at HEAD 9046abdc7f1b2e9d0fbf47a55ee636b94a631068 before writing this QA R2 handoff; worktree was clean.
R2 implementation commit changed only tests/sim-core-m2-economy-population.test.ts: 61 insertions and 48 deletions.

Verified R2 adds M2_ECONOMY_PROPERTY_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000 and passes it as the third Vitest test argument. The property workload remains numRuns=24, seed=1531, days 0-420, group ids 1-14, labor 1-80, and the existing population/labor/grain/cash/world-invariant assertions remain present.

R2 implementation-scope diff since the accepted content commit touched tests/sim-core-m2-economy-population.test.ts plus project process/handoff files only. No content, compiler, runtime, sim authority code, client, renderer, content-lock, or forbidden production scope changed in R2.

tests_run.result: Focused default command passed without CLI timeout override: 1 test file, 6 tests, duration 7.52s.

Default checked-in package command passed without reviewer-supplied timeout flags: 26 test files, 198 tests. Node emitted the existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Content validation passed. The default M2 prototype fixture remained districts=14, settlements=6, regionalSeasonalCurves=4, routes=25, mapGeometries=20, manifestHash=d474a105.
format:check, lint, typecheck, and architecture:check passed. Architecture self-test produced 15 expected violations.

decisions.decision: ACCEPT. The specific R1 blocker is fixed because the default checked-in sim-core package test passes without manual reviewer timeout flags.
R2 did not lower property workload or coverage: numRuns, seed, generated ranges, command path, and all non-negative population/labor/grain/cash and validateWorldStateV0 assertions are preserved.

R2 stayed within allowed paths and did not enter apps/web, apps/desktop, packages/ui, packages/map-renderer, content-lock, renderer, client, CI, roadmap, gate, manifest, checksum, or validation-report scope.

This acceptance covers only M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-FIXTURE-001 fixture/content/compiler/runtime/sim-core evidence. It does not approve M7 content lock, downstream client topology rendering, polygon hit testing, or M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-CLIENT-001.

risks.summary: The property test still advances 24 generated worlds and remains relatively expensive. R2 makes the default command reproducible by setting a narrow test timeout rather than reducing coverage.
Client rendering, polygon hit testing, player-facing route semantics, and map/list/task/inspector synchronization remain downstream work for M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-CLIENT-001 and later validation.
Formal M7 content lock remains blocked until the full topology recovery chain validates and the product owner explicitly reconsiders it.

requested_action: Accept M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-FIXTURE-001 R2 for the fixture/content/compiler/runtime/sim-core step and proceed with lead integration gates. Do not treat this as M7 content-lock approval and do not treat it as downstream client map rendering approval.
